3.18. Likewise, in line with the measurement principles set forth in Paragraphs 30
and 31 of IPSAS 17 –PPE, the cost of an asset at recognition must encompass
all expenditures necessary to bring the asset to a condition and location
suitable for its intended use. This includes, but is not limited to:
41
“xxx
Elements of Cost
30. The cost of an item of property, plant, and equipment comprises:
(a) Its purchase price, including import duties and non-refundable purchase
taxes, after deducting trade discounts and rebates.
(b) Any costs directly attributable to bringing the asset to the location and
condition necessary for it to be capable of operating in the manner intended
by management.
(c) The initial estimate of the costs of dismantling and removing the item
and restoring the site on which it is located, the obligation for which an
entity incurs either when the item is acquired, or as a consequence of
having used the item during a particular period for purposes other than to
produce inventories during that period.
31. Examples of directly attributable costs are:
(a) Costs of employee benefits (as defined in the relevant international or
national accounting standard dealing with employee benefits) arising
directly from the construction or acquisition of the item of property, plant,
and equipment;
(b) Costs of site preparation;
(c) Initial delivery and handling costs;
(d) Installation and assembly costs;
xxx”
3.19. Review of the PPE schedules submitted by the Office of the Municipal
Accountant disclosed that expenditures related to the completion of the Market
Building in CY 2024 were erroneously charged to Repair and Maintenance.
These include installations and improvements such as roll-up doors, electrical
wiring, lighting fixtures, outlets, and switches—all of which were necessary to
bring the asset into the condition required for it to operate as intended by
management. The improper classification of this completed project as outright
expenses, rather than capitalizing it under the appropriate PPE account,
resulted in significant misstatements in the financial statements.
3.20. As clearly established under Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17, an item of PPE should
be recognized as an asset if: (a) it is probable that future economic benefits or
service potential will flow to the entity; and (b) the cost or fair value of the item
can be measured reliably. The expenditures in question satisfy both criteria.
The Market Building and its related installations are expected to provide
continued service delivery and public utility, thereby generating service
potential beyond the current period. Accordingly, these assets should have
been capitalized at the time they became available for use.
3.21. Furthermore, Paragraphs 30 and 31 of IPSAS 17 outline that the cost of a PPE
asset includes not only its purchase price but also all expenditures directly
attributable to bringing the asset to the location and condition necessary for it to
function in accordance with management’s intent. These directly attributable
costs include installation and assembly, delivery, site preparation, and other
construction-related expenses. Thus, all costs incurred for the installations of
market fixtures and electrical components clearly fall under this definition and
should not have been charged to expense.

9.15. We recommended, and the management agreed to ensure compliance with the
prescribed COA Circular No. 2015-008 and to strengthen the accuracy,
completeness, and reliability of records for Road Network System account, as
follows:

a. Municipal Accountant
i. Establish and maintain LRNLCs for each component of the local road
network system to ensure proper monitoring, reconciliation, and reporting
of road infrastructure assets.
ii. Prepare and regularly update subsidiary records for roads and road
components for each road network, ensuring consistency with the
General Ledger and the Local Road Inventory.
iii. Develop a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation per road
component to ensure accurate financial reporting and proper valuation of
public infrastructure assets.

b. Municipal Treasurer/ GSO

i. Conduct and maintain a Local Road Inventory, ensuring that all road
components, including road lots, pavements, drainage systems, and other
structures, are properly recorded and accounted for.
ii. Secure a printed and regularly update the Road Map from the Municipal
Planning and Development Office (MPDO), which has been prepared
using the QGIS. This map should be updated periodically to reflect actual
and existing road networks for effective planning, maintenance, and
decision-making.
iii. Establish and maintain a LRNPC, providing a comprehensive record of all
roads and their components to facilitate asset tracking and accountability.

c. Municipal Engineer

i. Prepare and submit a detailed report on the Road Network System,
including a complete description of each road and a cost segregation
report that itemizes the value of each road component to ensure accurate
financial reporting and asset management.
ii. Provide the above report to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer/GSO for proper documentation and integration into financial
records.

9.16. We also recommended, and the LCE formally create a dedicated Inventory
Committee exclusively responsible for conducting the physical inventory of the
Road Network System. This committee should be composed of representatives
from key offices to ensure accountability, accuracy, and compliance with the
required reporting guidelines. Regular physical inventory-taking will aid in
reconciling records, preventing asset misstatements, and strengthening the
overall infrastructure management of the LGU.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the BRS on a timely basis and submit the same to the auditor together with the bank statements/snap shots and the negotiated or cleared checks.

It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to perform reconciliation between the cashbook and accounting records regularly.

It is also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct all AOs under his supervision to maintain cashbooks as part of internal control procedures in handling cash accounts.

It is recommended that management should close the remaining bank account maintained with PNB and transfer the amounts to a GFI with universal bank license and a CAMELS rating of at least 3 in accordance with Section 5.2 of DOF DC No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015.

It is recommended that the Municipality should file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances, which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to the above-mentioned regulation.

To address the conditions noted above and to improve the operation of the Municipality particularly in keeping of records to support the information reported in the financial statements, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and maintain the required cards and ledger cards.

It is recommended that after the conduct of the inventory, the Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the records and/or reports, investigate the variance noted, and make appropriate actions to address the results of the reconciliation and investigation.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expenses to all depreciable properties following the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2003-007 and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts to reflect the correct carrying value of the PPE.

It is recommended for the Municipality to enhance its compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:

Requiring the submission of the report of the Local Road Network by the GSO – Designate in order to implement and monitor the recognition of the account Local Road Network in the books of accounts;

Making proper disclosure in the Financial Statement of Road Network System; and
Exercising proper reconciliation between Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and make the necessary adjustment.

It is recommended that management shall insure all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. We also recommend for management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

It is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with updated Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations for the preparation of the SCBAA.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare the SCBAA and its required disclosure in compliance with PPSAS 24 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular 2016-004.

It is recommended that management shall appropriate the 20% DF to fund all projects that can contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

It is recommended that:

Management shall stop the practice of charging against the 20% EDF the expenditure which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development; and

Management shall strictly observe the guidelines provided by the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% EDF.

It is recommended that management shall ensure complete documentation of every transaction and adhere to the rules and regulations as provided above before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.
It is recommended that the MDRRMO will prepare and submit the required LDRRMFIP.

It is recommended that management shall approved disbursements with complete supporting documents. Personnel in-charge of performing pre-audit activities must be directed to carefully ascertain completeness of the required supporting documents before any claim is processed for payment.

It is recommended that the management exert extra effort in installing/maintaining a system of tax mapping by hiring additional personnel with expertise on the field to assists in reviewing all tax mapping documents to properly identify location, area, improvements, ownership and index number of properties.

It is recommended that management shall:

Craft and pass local ordinance regarding segregation at source with a sanctions and penalties for those who will violates the said ordinance; and

Conduct information, education and communication (IEC) campaign about proper ESWM and disseminate the approved local ordinance to the public.
It is recommended that the management shall provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste as required under RA No. 9003.

It is recommended that the municipality’s GAD Focal Point System shall conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the municipality in terms of GAD compliance.

To equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD, it also recommended that they shall undergo trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and

Revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 and other provisions of law.

Coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the PPAs implemented and to be implemented by the Municipality.

Reconciliation should be done of the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the MPDO in the preparation of PPAs report before submission to COA for audit validation.

Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and

Submit updated PPA report if there are corrections, revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for validation and monitoring.

We recommended that management accurately prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement of all bank accounts of the municipality on a timely basis and avoid further delay and submit to the auditor for review. Such reconciliation, to be accompanied with the bank statements together with the negotiated or cleared checks.

We recommended that management direct the relevant personnel to perform reconciliation between the treasurer’s records and accounting records regularly.

We recommended that management (a) complete the physical count, prepare properly the RPCPPE by accomplishing all columns in the form and indicating the whereabouts, condition and other relevant information relative to property, plant and equipment and submit the report within the prescribed period; and (b) regularly reconcile with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), General Ledger and Inventory Report including other property records like the updated Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE).

We recommended that management to provide depreciation for all depreciable assets.  Likewise, transfer completed and/or donated property, plant and equipment recorded in Trust Fund to General Fund amounting to P132.37 million in order that corresponding depreciation is recognized.
We recommended that management maintain subsidiary ledger to all accounts of the municipality as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

We recommended that management presents the noncurrent portion of the loans under Non-Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position and disclose them in details in the NTFS to provide clarity and divulge information that are readily unavailable on the face of the financial statements.

We recommended that the management direct all concerned officials and employees responsible in the grant of cash advances to disapprove all subsequent ones pending full accounting of the previous to avoid accumulation, issue demand letters to those with outstanding cash advances for its full settlement and liquidations, deduct from the salary to those with outstanding cash advances; and for those dormant accounts involving 10 years and above, require the filing of request for authority to write-off with the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management cease the practice on charging ineligible expenditures from LDRRMF. Likewise, undertakes extensive planning related to disaster risk reduction to preclude the existence of same conditions mentioned above.

We recommended that management fast-track the segregation of solid waste to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

We reiterated our recommendation that the municipality should impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, information and education campaign should be regularly conducted for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.

We recommended that management refrain from open burning of solid waste and strictly comply on various prohibitions of section 48 of RA 9003.

We reiterated our recommendation that management to check and provide personal protective equipment to those employees directly dealing with solid waste on a regular basis or when the need arises.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management exert extra effort in installing/maintaining a system of tax mapping by considering the hiring of additional personnel with expertise on the real property field assessment to assists in reviewing all tax mapping documents to properly identify location, area, improvements, ownership and index number of properties.

We recommended that the municipality’s GFPS shall conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; to equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD, they need to undergo trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9710 and other related provisions of law.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP), re-conduct evaluation and inspection on the municipal buildings’ fire safety standards and implement fire preventive measures like the creation of permanent stairways and/or improvise ladder to avoid the risk of losing lives and properties in case of man-made and/or natural fire calamities.

We recommended that the management shall provide firefighting equipment not only limited to fire sprinkler and fire alarm at the conspicuous places and formulate post emergency evacuation plan where anyone can have access.

We recommended that management removes all obstruction on passage ways/aisles and ensure that no hindrances exist on its fire exits, in compliance to the above cited provisions.

We reiterated our recommendation that management insure all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. We also recommended for management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS. Likewise, furnish this office the required Property Inventory Form not later than April 30 each year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Budget Officer provide the Municipal Accountant with updated Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations for the preparation of the SCBAA and that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, prepare the SCBAA and its required disclosure in compliance with PPSAS 24 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular 2016-004.

We reiterated our recommendation that management close the bank account maintained with Philippine National Bank and transfer the amounts to Government Financial Institutions with universal bank license and a CAMELS rating of at least 3.

We recommended that management investigate/trace the cause of the negative balance of the Cash in Bank account. Check if there were unrecorded cash receipts/inflows used to fund the disbursement. Validate as well the related disbursements which caused the overdraft if these were valid. Review the bank statement if there were unrecorded transactions. Effect the adjustment in the agency books when there is necessary adjusting entries to be made; and ensure that bank cashbook is updated and funds are available prior to the preparation and issuance of check for the purported disbursements.
We reiterated our recommendation that management provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.
It is recommended that Management shall determine the unexpended balances for QRF and MOOE as at December 31, 2018 and transfer the balance to the special trust fund for LDRRMF.
It is recommended that the MDRRMO shall ensure that the PAPs identified to be implemented within the year and funded under the 5% LDRRMF be effected and realized.

It is recommended that the MDRRMO will reconcile first the records between the MBO and the Municipal Accountant and see to it that correct and accurate amount are reflected in the Report on Utilization of LDRRMF.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of delinquent RPT by notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law and conduct information and education campaign on real property tax payment. Likewise, initiate measures to increase collection by implementing civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the BRS on a timely basis and submit the same to the auditor together with the bank statements/snap shots and the negotiated or cleared checks.
It is recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to perform reconciliation between the cashbook and accounting records regularly.
It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct all AOs under his supervision to maintain cashbooks as part of interal control procedures in handling cash accounts.

It is recommended that management should close the remaining bank account maintained with PNB and transfer the amounts to a GFI with universal bank license and a CAMELS rating of at least 3 in accordance with Section 5.2 of DOF DC No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015.

It is recommended that the Municipality should file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and unliquidated cash advances, which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to the above-mentioned regulation.

To address the conditions noted above and to improve the operation of the Municipality particularly in keeping of records to support the information reported in the financial statements, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and maintain the required cards and ledger cards.

It is recommended that after the conduct of the inventory, the Municipal Treasurer/Municipal Inventory Committee and Municipal Account shall reconcile the records and/or reports, investigate the variance noted, and make appropriate actions to address the results of the reconciliation and investigation.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expenses to all depreciable properties following the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2003-007 and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts to reflect the correct carrying value of the PPE.

It is recommended for the Municipality to enhance its compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:
Requiring the submission of the report of the Local Road Network by the GSO - Designate in order to implement and monitor the recognition of the account Local Road Network in the books of accounts;
Making proper disclosure in the Financial Statement of Road Network System; and
Exercising proper reconciliation between Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and make the necessary adjustment.

It is recommended that management shall insure all its insurable properties with the GSIS’ GIF in compliance with the above-cited provisions. We also recommend for management to conduct an inventory and appraisal of all insurable properties as required from the validation and review by the officials of the GSIS.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant will provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

It is recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with updated Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations for the preparation of the SCBAA.
It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare the SCBAA and its required disclosure in compliance with PPSAS 24 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular 2016-004.

It is recommended that management shall appropriate the 20% DF to fund all projects that can contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment of capital expenditures.

It is recommended that:
Management shall stop the practice of charging against the 20% EDF the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development; and
Management shall strictly observe the guidelines provided by the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% EDF.

It is recommended that management shall ensure complete documentation of every transaction and adhere to the rules and regulations as provided above before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.
It is recommended that the MDRRMO will prepare and submit the required LDRRMFIF.
It is recommended that Management shall determine the unexpended balances for QRF and MOOE as at December 31, 2018 and transfer the balance to the special trust fund for LDRRMF.
It is recommended that the MDRRMO shall ensure that the PAPs identified to be implemented within the year and funded under the 5% LDRRMF be effected and realized.

It is recommended that the MDRRMO will reconcile first the records between the MBO and the Municipal Accountant and see to it that correct and accurate amounts are reflected in the Report on Utilization of LDRRMF.

It is recommended that management shall approved disbursements with complete supporting documents. Personnel in-charge of performing pre-audit activities must be directed to carefully ascertain completeness of the required supporting documents before any claim is processed for payment.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of delinquent RPT by notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law and conduct information and education campaign on real property tax payment. Likewise, initiate measures to increase collection by implementing civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.

It is recommended that the management exert extra effort in installing/maintaining a system of tax mapping by hiring additional personnel with expertise on the field to assists in reviewing all tax mapping documents to properly identify location, area, improvements, ownership and index number of properties.

It is recommended that management shall:
Craft and pass local ordinance regarding segregation at source with a sanctions and penalties for those who will violates the said ordinance; and
Conduct information, education and communication (IEC) campaign about proper ESWM and disseminate the approved local ordinance to the public.
It is recommended that the management shall provide personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste as required under RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

      We  recommend  that  the  Municipality  enhance  its  compliance  with
policies and procedures in:

      a. Completing the actual physical count and the RPCPPE  and  reconcile
         the established differences to have accurate  Fixed  Asset  records
         and reports for the Municipality for which it is  held  accountable
         and provide adequate disclosures to guide the users of information;


      b. Maintaining proper Fixed Asset records for all Municipality’s fixed
         assets;


      c. Assigning Fixed Asset to end user or the least the head  of  office
         having custody of the assets  to  facilitate  easy  monitoring  for
         inventory purposes and ensure that the  same  are  being  kept  and
         safeguarded by responsible personnel


      d. Taking proper action and procedure  to  dispose  all  unserviceable
         property in order to obtain a reasonable auction bid and avoid  the
         losing of an opportunity to exploit a return from its disposal.


Management Comment:

      The Municipality agree with the finding and will implement  the  same.
They commit to revisit the previously created inventory committee  and  will
make the necessary revision that includes  different  heads  of  offices  to
facilitate the conduct of inventory per office and  eventually  to  come  up
with a consolidated report on the result of physical count.

      The further commented that they will conduct inventory  a  week  after
the conduct of the audit exit  conference  and  to  finished  the  inventory
taking within one month.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Municipal Government enhance its compliance with
policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:

        a. Taking up depreciation expense on the depreciable components  of
           Road Network account;


        b. Requiring the submission of the report of the Local Road Network
           by the GSO/Municipal Treasurer in order to implement and monitor
           the recognition of the account Local Road Network in  the  books
           of accounts;


        c. Improving the maintenance of subsidiary ledger for the Road  and
           it components;


        d. Making proper disclosure in  the  Financial  Statement  of  Road
           Network System;

        e. Exercising proper reconciliation between Report on the  Physical
           Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network  Ledger  Card
           and make the necessary adjustment;


        f. Providing cost to specific Road Network in order to have  proper
           valuation and completeness of the Road Network Account.








Management Comment:

      The Municipality agree with the finding and will implement  the  same.
They informed that they will prepare the Road Map in order to come  up  with
the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network  and  other  required
reports.

Recommendation:

      The Municipality through the Municipal  Mayor  should  require  strict
compliance in allocating 1% of  its  Internal  Revenue  Allotment  (IRA)  as
provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344 and the  LGU’s  Local  Council  for
the Protection of Children should adopt a comprehensive plan on  delinquency
prevention, and oversee its proper implementation.

Management Comment:

      The  Municipality  agree  with  the  finding  and  recommendation  and
informed that while it is true that no specific funding is reflected in  the
Annual Budget but they can identify programs  and  projects  in  the  annual
budget that are aligned with RA 9344 by way of attribution.

      They further commented that  will  prepare  a  comprehensive  plan  on
child delinquency prevention for CY 2018.

Recommendation:


      We recommend  that  the  municipality  should  file  the  request  for
authority to write-off dormant receivables and  unliquidated  cash  advances
which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected  pursuant  to
COA Circular No 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.


Management Comment:

      The Municipality explained that they will exert effort to collect  and
will consider to request  for  authority  to  write-off  if  conditions  and
requirements are met.

Recommendation:


      The Municipality  should  implement  the  segregation  at  source  and
segregated collection to  complete  the  implementation  of  the  Ecological
solid Waste Management System.


Management Comment:


       The  Municipality  informed  that  they  will   conduct   information
dissemination through general assembly and will impose “  no  collection  if
no segregation” policy. They also informed they  have  plan  to  purchase  a
compactor to be used for the collection of garbage.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the management of the Municipality of Gutalac should
insure all insurable assets with the GSIS to  cover  for  the  cost  of  the
properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent  event,  and  to
comply with Section 489 of the Government  Accounting  and  Auditing  Manual
Volume I and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 .

Management Comment:

      The Municipality  agrees  with  the  finding  and  recommendation  and
commits to implement the same.

Recommendation:


      We recommend that  management  should  close  the  two  bank  accounts
maintained with  Philippine  National  Bank  and  transfer  the  amounts  to
Government Financial Institutions with universal bank license and  a  CAMELS
rating of at least 3  in  accordance  with  Section  5.2  of  Department  of
Finance (DOF) Department Circular (DC) No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015.

Management Comment:

       The  Municipality  informed  that  PNB  Liloy   account   cannot   be
transferred to GFI until the existing  loan  of  the  Municipality  will  be
fully paid. They further informed that for PNB Sindangan  Branch  they  will
facilitate the transfer to GFI immediately.

Recommendation:

      The Municipality shall strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions  of
COA No. 2012-002 on the reporting of LDRRMF by:

   1. Requiring the accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the  Receipt
      and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM)  Fund
      and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster  Risk  Reduction
      and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month;


   2.  Submitting  immediately  the  Local  Disaster  Risk   Reduction   and
      Management Investment Fund (LDRRMFIP) as required under  COA  Circular
      No. 2012-002 indicating the 30% allocation for QRF and the  allocation
      for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with  details  as
      to projects and activities to be funded; and


   3. Accelerating the filling –up of the required staff under the LDRRMO.

   4. The unexpended LDRRMF shall be transferred to the Special  Trust  Fund
      and the concerned personnel shall ensure  that  the  total  unexpended
      balance of QRF and LDRRMF - MOOE will be transferred  to  the  Special
      Trust Fund at the end of the year.


Management Comment:


      The municipality informed that they will transfer  the  unused  LDRRMF
from the General Fund to Trust Fund and they  will  comply  with  the  staff
requirements in the Office of the LDRRMO.

Recommendations:

        We recommend that the  Municipality  enhance  its  compliance  with
   policies and procedures in:

        a.  Completing  the  actual  physical  count  and  the  RPCPPE  and
           reconcile the established differences  to  have  accurate  Fixed
           Asset records and reports for the Municipality for which  it  is
           held accountable and provide adequate disclosures to  guide  the
           users of information;


        b. Maintaining proper Fixed Asset records  for  all  Municipality’s
           fixed assets;


        c. Assigning Fixed Asset to end user  or  the  least  the  head  of
           office  having  custody  of  the  assets  to   facilitate   easy
           monitoring for inventory purposes and ensure that the  same  are
           being kept and safeguarded by responsible personnel


        d. Taking proper action and procedure to dispose all  unserviceable
           property in order to obtain a reasonable auction bid  and  avoid
           the losing of an  opportunity  to  exploit  a  return  from  its
           disposal.

We recommend that management should close  the  two  bank  accounts
   maintained with Philippine National Bank  and  transfer  the  amounts  to
   Government Financial Institutions  with  universal  bank  license  and  a
   CAMELS rating of at least 3 in accordance with Section 5.2 of  Department
   of Finance (DOF) Department Circular (DC)  No.  001-2015  dated  June  1,
   2015.

We recommend that the management of  the  Municipality  of  Gutalac
   should insure all insurable assets with the GSIS to cover for the cost of
   the properties in case of loss due to fire and  other  contingent  event,
   and to comply with Section 489 of the Government Accounting and  Auditing
   Manual Volume I and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 .

We recommend that  the  LSB  should  refrain  from  providing  non-
   priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for  those  that  are
   part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead,  it
   should be allocate SEF funds for the construction and  repair  of  school
   buildings; facilities and equipment; education research;  acquisition  of
   books and periodicals; and sports development.

The  Municipality should adequately enhance its collection effort or
   institute collection process through the available remedies  as  provided
   by the Local Government Code.

1. Coordination must be established between  the  Bids  and  Awards
           Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal  Budget  Office  and
           the Municipal Planning  and  Development  Office  regarding  the
           preparation of the report on the  PPAs  implemented  and  to  be
           implemented by the Municipality.


        2. Reconciliation should be done of the records maintained  by  the
           BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the MPDO
           in the preparation of PPAs report before submission to  COA  for
           audit validation.


        3. Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering  Office  to
           submit at the beginning of  the  year  in  accordance  with  the
           provision of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January  30,
           2013; and


        4. Submit updated PPA report if there are corrections, revisions or
           changes to the projects to be  implemented  for  validation  and
           monitoring.

The Municipality should implement the  segregation  at  source  and
   segregated collection to complete the implementation  of  the  Ecological
   solid Waste Management System.

We recommend that the unexpended LDRRMF shall be transferred to the
   Special Trust Fund and the concerned  personnel  shall  ensure  that  the
   total unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRMF - MOOE will be transferred  to
   the Special Trust Fund at the end of the year.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  immediately  effect
   necessary adjustments to correct the excessive contributions made by  the
   municipality in its employer’s share to all  employees  monthly  Pag-IBIG
   premium contributions in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9679.

We recommend that an inventory committee will be created to conduct physical count, and consider the designated Property Officer to be the Chairman of the Committee. Likewise, direct the Inventory Committee to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property as basis for the accounting unit to recognize impairment losses and for its disposal.

The Municipal Accountant should maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory stock, and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of plant, property and equipment. Further, require the designated Property Officer to update the stock and property cards maintained for inventories and property, plant and equipment. Reconciliation on their records is to be made by both the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant.
